suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add guidance that doctor subcommands should include CLI integration tests (using `crate::cli::run()` with tempdir fixtures), not just inline unit tests. The governance-consistency feature only has unit tests while the sibling ownership command has proper integration tests.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: doctor-namespace-intent review: src/cli/doctor/governance_consistency.rs tests only call detect_governance_conflicts() directly, while src/cli/doctor/ownership.rs tests use crate::cli::run() with tempdir fixtures for end-to-end CLI verification.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Clarify doctor submodule visibility conventions: new doctor submodules should be declared as private `mod` (not `pub(crate) mod`) and expose external types through dedicated modules (e.g., src/spec/) if needed, following the ownership pattern.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: doctor-namespace-intent review: src/cli/doctor/mod.rs line 4 declares `pub(crate) mod governance_consistency` while all other doctor submodules are private `mod`. The ownership module achieves external access via src/spec/ownership.rs instead.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Remind workers that adding new error-level diagnostic findings to doctor subcommands should be accompanied by a README update to the relevant configuration option description, per the existing AGENTS.md rule about updating operator docs when CLI output changes.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: doctor-ownership-globs review: README.md describes strict_ownership_level: errors as gating 'duplicate module ids and invalid ownership globs' but contradictory_globs is now also error-level. AGENTS.md rule: 'If a change affects CLI flags, verdict wording, or install/release behavior, update the operator docs in README.md in the same patch.'
